title: What, Where, When, Who, Why, How
description: The six wh- words that open every question. Plus the word-order trap (subject-verb inversion).
summary_pt_br: Seis palavras-chave de pergunta: WHAT/WHERE/WHEN/WHO/WHY/HOW. Regra: wh- + AUXILIAR + SUJEITO + verbo. 'Where DO YOU live?' (não 'where you live?').

scenes.text_en:
WHAT do you like?
WHERE do you live?
WHEN does class start?
WHO is that person?
WHY are you tired?
HOW do you feel?
Six words. Six questions. Always the same order: WH + AUX + SUBJECT + VERB.
scenes.text_pt:
O QUE você gosta?
ONDE você mora?
QUANDO começa a aula?
QUEM é aquela pessoa?
POR QUE você tá cansado?
COMO você tá?
Seis palavras. Seis perguntas. Sempre: WH + AUXILIAR + SUJEITO + VERBO.

scenes.title: Order + auxiliary rule
scenes.body_en: Wh- + AUX + subject + main verb. 'Where DO YOU live?' With BE, the auxiliary IS the verb: 'Where ARE YOU from?' Past: 'Where DID YOU go?' Never 'Where you live?' — always need the auxiliary. Exception: 'WHO' as subject keeps statement order: 'Who called you?' (who = subject).
scenes.body_pt: Wh- + AUX + sujeito + verbo. 'Where do you live?'. Com BE, o próprio verbo é o auxiliar: 'Where are you?'. Nunca 'Where you live?'. Exceção: WHO como sujeito mantém ordem afirmativa ('Who called?').

examples.en: What do you do? / Where are you? / When did she leave?

exercise.question: Complete: '___ ___ your name?'
exercise.explanation: With BE, the verb comes right after the wh-word.

scenes.title: The reporter interview
scenes.pt_summary: Question words — Who / What / Where / When / Why / How.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Hi. I am a reporter. Can I ask you some questions?
turns.pt: Oi. Sou repórter. Posso te fazer perguntas?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Sure. What do you want to know?

turns.speaker: ai
turns.pt: Quando você se mudou pra lá?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: I moved there two years ago.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Why did you choose São Paulo?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Because I got a new job there.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: How do you feel about the city?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: I feel great. I love it.

scenes.title: Listening story — What, Where, When, Who, Why, How
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça a situação com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que você entendeu — resumo, detalhes, seus pensamentos. O professor vai ler e comentar.
scenes.prompt_en: Write in English what you heard. A short summary is fine. Mention who spoke, what they said, and what you think about the situation.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que você ouviu. Pode ser um resumo curto. Diga quem falou, o que disseram e o que você achou.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: In this story, we meet Reporter and a student. The six wh- words that open every question. Plus the word-order trap (subject-verb inversion). Listen carefully and think about what each person says.

paragraphs.speaker: Reporter
paragraphs.en: Hi. I am a reporter. Can I ask you some questions?

paragraphs.speaker: The student
paragraphs.en: Sure. What do you want to know?

paragraphs.speaker: The student
paragraphs.en: I moved there two years ago.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: That is the end of our short scene with Reporter. Think about what you heard and how the conversation felt.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "What, Where, When, Who, Why, How". The six wh- words that open every question. Plus the word-order trap (subject-verb inversion). Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up. The ocean in Praia do Sol keeps moving, and so does your English.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: A chave é a prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever seus próprios exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "What, Where, When, Who, Why, How". I liked it because it was clear and short. Tomorrow we will practice again. See you then!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule about the past
What, Where, When, Who, Why, How
check.explanation: She says: "Today in class we studied 'What, Where, When, Who, Why, How'".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala claramente o título: "What, Where, When, Who, Why, How".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para você estudar mais. Salve os links e volte sempre.
